identity.name: Hvar
identity.description: Hvar is a Croatian island in the Adriatic Sea, located off the Dalmatian coast, lying between the islands of Brač, Vis and Korčula. Approximately 68 kilometres (42.25 mi) long, with a high east–west ridge of Mesozoic limestone and dolomite, the island of Hvar is unusual in the area for having a large fertile coastal plain, and fresh water springs. Its hillsides are covered in pine forests, with vineyards, olive groves, fruit orchards and lavender fields in the agricultural areas.

osm_tags.wheelchair:description: Bar ist in Stufen aufgebaut  Eingang von oben (nicht von der Promenade)

osm_tags.note: Dinner is available by prior reservation, at least one day in advance. Without a reservation, we offer a daily menu.

osm_tags.description: Duplex Apartment 143 sq. metres with terrace and courtyard

The house is situated in a quiet part of Jelsa, 80 meters from the sea. There are several pebble beaches. Around the house there are gardens with many Mediterranean plants, such as olives, figs, pomegranates, lemons, rosemary ... and by the house is a pin

osm_tags.name:it: Chiesetta dei Santi Pietro e Paolo

osm_tags.inscription: Εορταζοντας 2400 χρόνια από την ίδρυση της Φάρου, αποικιας των Παριων στο χροατικό νηοι τον Hvar, οι δυο λαοί του Hvar και της Παριου ανεγείρουν αυτό το μνημείο εις ενδειξιν φιλίας και συνεργασίας.
